questions.q: What is the output type of the following TypeScript expression?    `typeof null`
options: null
object
undefined
string

questions.q: Which utility type makes all properties of a type optional?
options: Required<T>
Readonly<T>
Partial<T>
Pick<T, K>

questions.q: What does the `never` type represent in TypeScript?
options: A value that is always undefined
A type that cannot have any value
An empty object type
A type for null values

questions.q: Which of the following correctly defines a generic function?
options: function id(x: T): T { return x; }
function id<T>(x: T): T { return x; }
function id<T>(x): T { return x; }
function<T> id(x: T): T { return x; }

questions.q: What is a discriminated union in TypeScript?
options: A union with duplicate types removed
A union type with a common literal property used for narrowing
A union that only allows primitive types
A union restricted to two types

questions.q: What does `keyof` do in TypeScript?
options: Returns all values of an object type
Returns the keys of an object as a union type
Iterates over object keys at runtime
Removes keys from an object type

questions.q: Which of the following is true about `interface` vs `type` in TypeScript?
options: type can be extended; interface cannot
interface supports declaration merging; type does not
type cannot represent object shapes
interface can represent primitive types directly

questions.q: What is the result of `'hello' as unknown as number` in TypeScript?
options: Compilation error
Runtime error
Allowed — compiles without error
Returns NaN at runtime

questions.q: What does `Readonly<T>` do?
options: Freezes the object at runtime
Makes all properties of T read-only at compile time
Prevents object reassignment
Removes all methods from T

questions.q: What is the purpose of the `infer` keyword in TypeScript?
options: To create runtime type checks
To declare a type variable inside a conditional type
To infer generic types automatically
To create intersection types

questions.q: Which of these correctly narrows a union type using a type guard?
options: if (x is string) { }
if (typeof x === 'string') { }
if (x.type === string) { }
if (x instanceof 'string') { }

questions.q: What does `Record<K, V>` represent?
options: An array of key-value pairs
A mapped type with keys of type K and values of type V
A tuple with two elements
A readonly map

questions.q: What happens when you use `strictNullChecks: true`?
options: null is removed from all types
undefined and null are excluded from every type unless explicitly included
Only null is excluded from types
Strict mode is automatically enabled

questions.q: What is the difference between `unknown` and `any`?
options: They are identical in behavior
unknown requires type narrowing before use; any does not
any is stricter than unknown
unknown can be assigned to any type without narrowing

questions.q: Which TypeScript feature allows you to define types based on the shape of a value?
options: typeof operator
instanceof check
Mapped types
Template literal types

questions.q: What does `Extract<T, U>` do?
options: Removes from T all types that are assignable to U
Extracts from T all types that are assignable to U
Returns the intersection of T and U
Picks properties from T that match U

questions.q: What is the correct way to define a tuple type in TypeScript?
options: type T = Array<string, number>
type T = [string, number]
type T = (string | number)[]
type T = {0: string; 1: number}

questions.q: What does the `satisfies` operator do in TypeScript?
options: Asserts a type at runtime
Validates that an expression matches a type while preserving its narrowed type
Converts a type to another type
Creates a type assertion

questions.q: Which utility type removes `null` and `undefined` from a type?
options: Exclude<T, null>
Required<T>
NonNullable<T>
Defined<T>

questions.q: What is structural typing in TypeScript?
options: Types checked by their name
Types checked by their shape/structure
Types validated at runtime
Types defined using classes only

questions.q: What does `Awaited<T>` resolve to?
options: The type T wrapped in a Promise
The resolved type of a Promise<T>
The type of T after awaiting once
The union of T and Promise<T>

questions.q: What is a mapped type in TypeScript?
options: An array of key-value pairs
A type that transforms each property of another type
A type created using Map<K, V>
A function type mapping inputs to outputs

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a valid TypeScript access modifier?
options: public
private
protected
internal

questions.q: What does `ReturnType<T>` extract?
options: The parameter types of a function
The return type of a function type T
The constructor type of a class
The resolved type of a promise

questions.q: What is a conditional type in TypeScript?
options: A type that depends on runtime conditions
A type expression of the form T extends U ? X : Y
A type that changes based on compiler flags
A union type with conditions

questions.q: What does the `override` keyword ensure in TypeScript?
options: A method must be declared in the class itself
A method must override a base class method, catching rename errors
A method cannot be further overridden
A method is called before the parent method

questions.q: What are template literal types used for?
options: Formatting strings at runtime
Creating new string types by combining literal types
Declaring multiline strings
Replacing string interpolation

questions.q: What is the difference between `==` and `===` in TypeScript?
options: No difference — TypeScript treats them identically
== performs type coercion; === checks value and type strictly
=== is only for objects
== is not allowed in TypeScript

questions.q: What does `Parameters<T>` return?
options: The return type of T
A tuple of parameter types of function type T
An array of parameter names
The number of parameters of T

questions.q: Which of the following is true about enums in TypeScript?
options: All enums are purely compile-time constructs
Const enums are erased at compile time; regular enums generate JS code
Enums cannot have string values
Enums are identical to union types

questions.q: What does the `abstract` keyword mean on a class?
options: The class cannot have any methods
The class cannot be instantiated directly and may have abstract methods
The class is marked for deletion
The class properties are all optional

questions.q: What is an index signature in TypeScript?
options: A way to sort object keys
A syntax to define types for dynamic property names
A method to access array indices
A reference to object prototype

questions.q: Which of the following correctly implements a type predicate?
options: function isString(x: any): boolean { return typeof x === 'string'; }
function isString(x: any): x is string { return typeof x === 'string'; }
function isString(x: any): typeof string { return typeof x === 'string'; }
function isString(x: string): boolean { return true; }

questions.q: What does `Omit<T, K>` do?
options: Picks only the properties K from T
Removes the properties K from T
Makes properties K optional in T
Extracts K from a union T

questions.q: What is declaration merging in TypeScript?
options: Combining two .ts files into one
When multiple declarations with the same name are merged into one definition
Merging type and value namespaces
Combining generic types

questions.q: What is the output of: `type T = true extends boolean ? 'yes' : 'no'`?
options: 'no'
boolean
'yes'
never

questions.q: Which TypeScript feature allows pattern matching on string types?
options: Conditional types
Template literal types
Mapped types
Index signatures

questions.q: What does enabling `noImplicitAny` do?
options: Disallows the any type entirely
Raises an error when a variable implicitly gets an any type
Removes all any types from the codebase
Converts any to unknown automatically

questions.q: What is the correct syntax for a type assertion in TypeScript?
options: x as Type or <Type>x (in non-JSX files)
x: Type
Type(x)
x satisfies Type

questions.q: Which of the following is true about `readonly` arrays?
options: Elements can be reassigned but not added
Neither mutation methods (push, pop) nor index assignment are allowed
They are identical to regular arrays at runtime
readonly arrays are converted to tuples

questions.q: What does `ConstructorParameters<T>` return?
options: The instance type of a class
A tuple of parameter types of a constructor function type
The prototype of a class
A list of class methods

questions.q: Which of the following correctly uses a namespace in TypeScript?
options: import Validation from './validation'
namespace Validation { export interface StringValidator { } }
module Validation { export interface StringValidator { } }
package Validation { interface StringValidator { } }

questions.q: What is covariance in TypeScript's type system?
options: A type can be substituted with a more general type
A type can be substituted with a more specific (narrower) subtype
Two types are unrelated
Types are checked at runtime

questions.q: What does `Exclude<T, U>` do?
options: Extracts union members of T assignable to U
Removes from T all union members assignable to U
Intersects T and U
Returns types in U not present in T

questions.q: Which of the following is true about function overloads in TypeScript?
options: Each overload must have a separate function body
Overload signatures are for type checking; the implementation signature handles the actual logic
Overloads are not supported in TypeScript
Overloads must have the same parameter count

questions.q: What is an ambient declaration in TypeScript?
options: A declaration inside a namespace
A declaration that tells TypeScript about types that exist elsewhere (e.g., .d.ts files)
A declaration with default values
A declaration that's removed at compile time

questions.q: What does `InstanceType<T>` return?
options: The constructor function type of a class
The instance type of a constructor function type T
The prototype of T
The static members of a class

questions.q: Which option enables strict null checks, noImplicitAny, and several other strict options at once?
options: noImplicitAny: true
strictMode: true
strict: true
fullStrict: true

questions.q: What is the purpose of `declare module` in TypeScript?
options: To create a new module at runtime
To augment or describe the types of an existing module
To import a module dynamically
To mark a module as deprecated

questions.q: What is the `using` keyword introduced in TypeScript 5.2?
options: A shorthand for importing modules
A way to declare variables that are automatically disposed via Symbol.dispose
A syntax for using generics inline
A keyword for defining mixins
